titleStmt.title: SII 23.421: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 421. (A.R. No. 421 of 1907.) Kambam, Periyakulam Taluk, Madurai District Subrahmaṇya temple—on the south wall Jaṭ. Vīra-Pāṇḍya I: year 10: 1262-63 A.D.

summary.p: The king is given the distinguishing epithets ‘who took Īḻam, Koṅgu and Śōḻamaṇḍalam, and having conquered the Vallāṉ (powerful enemy ?) performed the anointment of heroes and victors at Tillaimānagar’. The inscription records a gift of tax-free land (by order of the king ?) for offerings to god Śēvagattēvar Tirchcheṇḍupiḍitta-Piḷḷaiyār (Subrahmaṇya ?) and goddess Periyapirāṭṭiyār at Tiruppuduvūr in Toṇḍaimāṉ-vaḷanāḍu.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . koccaṭaiyavaṉmarāṉa tribhuvaṉacca
kravattikaḷ Īḻamuṅ koṅkuñ coḻamaṇṭalamuṅ
koṇṭu vallāṉai veṉṟu tillaimāṉakaril vi
ṟṟiruntu virābhiṣekamum vijayābhiṣekamum paṇṇi
yaruḷiya śrīvirapāṇḍyadevaṟku yāṇṭu 10vatu Āṉi mā
tam tirunilakaṇṭaṉ tirunilakaṇṭaṉāṉa toṇṭaimā
ṉvaḷanāṭṭut tirupputuvūr Ittirumuṟṟattukkuṭaiyaān E
ḻuntaruḷivitta cevakattevarāṉa tirucceṇṭu piṭittappiḷ
ḷaiyāṉa periyapirāṭṭiyāṟkum tiruppoṉakattu
kkum maṟṟuḷḷa veñcaṉaṅkaḷukkum Innāṭṭuc civa
māṇṭāṉerik kuḷakkiḻ teṟkil maṭaipokkil mu
ṉ naṭuvaṭaiya patiṉāṟaṭik kolāl kāṭṭik kuṭutta nilam
māvukkum Epperpaṭṭa Iṟaiyun tavintu nālellai ka
llum nāṭṭikkoṇṭu candrādityavat Ittaṉmam
tom tirunilakaṇṭaṉ tirunilakaṇṭaṉāṉa toṇ
Ippaṭikku Ivai cempiyataraiyaṉ Eḻuttu I
vai cetirāyaṉ Eḻuttu Ippaṭikku Ivai
Digital edition of SII 23.421 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
